name: Return to the Sierra del Tallat - Circular from Bellpuig
name_original: Vuelta a la Sierra del Tallat - Circular desde Bellpuig
region_intro: Road cycling around Bellpuig is characterized by a mix of flat plains and gently undulating landscapes, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is primarily defined by the flat Ponent plain, irrigated by the Urgell Canal, which provides ideal conditions for gentle rides. Towards the Segre river, the land becomes slightly undulating, while the nearby Sierra del Coscollar offers more varied topography and greater elevation gain for experienced cyclists. This combination ensures a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Bellpuig The most popular road cycling route is Return to the Sierra del Tallat - Circular from Bellpuig, a 60.1 miles (96.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Forès Pass – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Bellpuig, a difficult 47.9 miles (77.2 km) path. This route features challenging climbs and descends, passing through rural landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Bellpuig Castle – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Bellpuig, a 22.4 miles (36.1 km) trail leading through historical sites and gentle hills, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Road cycling around Bellpuig is defined by the flat agricultural plains, the scenic Urgell Canal, and the more challenging ascents of the Sierra del Coscollar. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to demanding climbs. The routes in Bellpuig are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bellpuig's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Bellpuig?
A: Bellpuig offers a diverse mix of terrain for road cyclists. The region is primarily characterized by the flat Ponent plain, irrigated by the Urgell Canal, which is ideal for gentle rides. Towards the Segre river, the land becomes gently undulating, while the nearby Sierra del Coscollar provides more varied topography and greater elevation gain for experienced cyclists.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bellpuig?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Bellpuig, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bellpuig?
A: Yes, Bellpuig offers routes suitable for beginners. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route available. The flat agricultural plains and sections along the Urgell Canal are particularly well-suited for those new to road cycling or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes near Bellpuig?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes heading towards the Sierra del Coscollar offer significant elevation gain. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939000146/return-to-the-sierra-del-tallat-circular-from-bellpuig" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Sierra del Tallat - Circular from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 96.7 km route with over 1200 meters of ascent, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10426783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forès Pass – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 77.2 km path with over 1000 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there long-distance road cycling options in the Bellpuig area?
A: Yes, Bellpuig is a great starting point for long-distance road cycling. Several routes extend beyond 90 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939000146/return-to-the-sierra-del-tallat-circular-from-bellpuig" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Sierra del Tallat - Circular from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt; (96.7 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33847436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Omells de na Gaia – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt; (96.5 km), offering extensive rides through varied landscapes.

Q: What scenic attractions or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Bellpuig?
A: Many routes pass through picturesque agricultural plains and along the scenic Urgell Canal. You can also explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5616171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tàrrega&lt;/a&gt;, or ride past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de Almenara Tower&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of San Vicente de Almenara&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also feature views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring historical sites in Bellpuig?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history, and many road cycling routes are designed to pass through or near historical villages and landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40364525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellpuig Castle – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt; takes you through historical sites and gentle hills, offering a cultural cycling experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Bellpuig?
A: The road cycling routes around Bellpuig are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the flat plains along the Urgell Canal to the more challenging ascents of the Sierra del Coscollar, and the opportunity to explore rural landscapes and historical sites.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Bellpuig?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Bellpuig are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939000146/return-to-the-sierra-del-tallat-circular-from-bellpuig" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Sierra del Tallat - Circular from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Albió – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Bellpuig&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Bellpuig?
A: The Ponent plain generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summer can be warm, especially in the flat plains, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winter cycling is also an option, though you should check local weather forecasts for cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that offer views of lakes or natural parks?
A: While Bellpuig is primarily known for its agricultural plains, some routes may offer glimpses of local water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural highlight in the broader area, and routes passing through the region might offer views of this or other irrigation ponds, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt;.

The Tallat sanctuary is located on top of a 788-meter-high hill in the homonymous mountain range. It is Gothic in style and dates from the 13th century. History of the Civil War.

The itinerary starts from the village of Vallbona de les Monges where the only female monastery of this itinerary stands. The first news of the monastery dates back to 1153 and was created in the unmistakable Gothic style that characterizes the main religious buildings in the region. Here, in the period of greatest splendor, up to 150 nuns came to gather, all from wealthy families.
